===On hand===
{{TrainerPoké
|main=yes
|trainer=Zoey
|pkmn=Glameow
|type1=Normal
|img=Zoey Glameow.png
|epnum=DP011
|epname=Mounting a Coordinator Assault!
|vajp=Ikue Otani
|vaen=Ikue Otani
|desc={{p|Glameow}} is Zoey's main Pokémon. It was first seen in ''[[DP011|Mounting a Coordinator Assault!]]'' when it approached {{an|Dawn}} with her missing choker around its tail. In the [[DP012|next episode]] it was used in the {{ci|Jubilife}} {{pkmn|Contest}}, and managed to take out [[Ash's Aipom]], [[Dawn's Buneary]], and [[Jessie]]'s borrowed {{TP|James|Carnivine}}, earning Zoey the Jubilife [[Ribbon]].

Zoey used Glameow in almost every Contest she participated in.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Zoey
|pkmn=Mismagius
|stage1=Misdreavus
|type1=Ghost
|img=Zoey Misdreavus.png
|cap=Zoey's Misdreavus
|img2=Zoey Mismagius.png
|cap2=Zoey's Mismagius
|epnum=DP011
|epname=Mounting a Coordinator Assault!
|vajp=Miyako Itō
|vaen=Kayzie Rogers
|vaennote=as Misdreavus
|vaen2=Sarah Natochenny
|vaen2note=as Mismagius
|desc={{p|Mismagius}} made its debut in the start of the {{ci|Jubilife}} {{pkmn|Contest}} in ''[[DP011|Mounting a Coordinator Assault!]]'' as a {{p|Misdreavus}}. It made its appeal by coming out of its {{i|Poké Ball}} covered in a purple fog. It was silhouetted in the mist making only its shadow visible, thus making it look much more ominous and frightening.

Misdreavus was later seen in ''[[DP041|A Stand-Up Sit-Down!]]'' searching for someone to help Zoey alongside {{TP|Zoey|Glameow}}. It was also seen defeating a {{p|Mothim}} in the [[Wallace Cup]] in ''[[DP078|Pruning a Passel of Pals!]]''.

In ''[[DP176|Coming Full Festival Circle!]]'', it was revealed to have evolved into a Mismagius since the Wallace Cup. Zoey used it along with her {{p|Leafeon}} against [[Nando]] in the semifinals of the [[Sinnoh Grand Festival]] where they helped Zoey advance to the final round.

Mismagius's known moves are {{m|Double Team}}, {{m|Confuse Ray}}, {{m|Shock Wave}}{{tt|*|Dub incorrectly referred to this as Thunder Wave in DP176}}, {{m|Lucky Chant}}, and {{m|Psywave}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|main=yes
|trainer=Zoey
|pkmn=Gastrodon
|stage1=Shellos
|type1=Water
|type2=Ground
|img=Zoey Shellos.png
|img2=Zoey Gastrodon.png
|epnum=DP041
|epname=A Stand-Up Sit-Down!
|vajp=Megumi Hayashibara
|vaen=Sarah Natochenny
|vaennote=as Shellos
|vaen2=Tom Wayland
|vaen2note=as Gastrodon
|desc=Zoey caught {{p|Gastrodon}} as a {{p|Shellos}} shortly after the events of ''[[DP034|Buizel Your Way Out of This]]''. After failing to capture {{AP|Buizel}}, she stayed in the area to find another {{type|Water}} Pokémon. Shellos then made its first appearance in ''[[DP041|A Stand-Up Sit-Down!]]''. It was seen at the {{ci|Hearthome}} {{pkmn|Contest}} in ''[[DP049|Dawn's Early Night]]''.

In ''[[DP174|Last Call, First Round!]]'', it was revealed to have evolved into a Gastrodon, used by Zoey in the appeals round and later the first battle round of the Grand Festival, where it easily helped her pass both stages.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Zoey
|pkmn=Lumineon
|stage1=Finneon
|type1=Water
|img=Zoey Finneon.png
|cap=Zoey's Finneon

|epname=Staging a Heroes' Welcome!
|vajp=
|vaen=Michele Knotz
|vaennote=as Finneon
|desc={{p|Lumineon}} as a {{p|Finneon}} first appeared in ''[[DP077|Staging a Heroes' Welcome!]]''. Zoey sent it out to perform in the [[Appeal|Performance Stage]] of the [[Wallace Cup]], and its performance was impressive enough to advance Zoey to the next round.

In ''[[DP174|Last Call—First Round!]]'', it was revealed to have evolved into a Lumineon. Zoey used it along with her Gastrodon in the Performance Stage and first battle round, where its grace and beauty easily helped her pass both stages.

Lumineon's known moves are {{m|Silver Wind}}, {{m|Safeguard}}, {{m|Waterfall}}, and {{m|Aqua Ring}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Zoey
|pkmn=Gallade
|stage1=Kirlia
|gender=male
|type1=Psychic
|type2=Fighting
|img=Zoey Kirlia.png
|cap=Zoey's Kirlia
|img2=Zoey Gallade.png
|cap2=Zoey's Gallade
|epnum=DP155
|epname=Double-Time Battle Training!
|vajp=Satsuki Yukino
|vaen=Michele Knotz
|vaennote=as Kirlia
|vaen2=Tom Wayland
|vaen2note=as Gallade
|desc={{p|Gallade}} first appeared in ''[[DP155|Double-Time Battle Training!]]'', where he defeated [[Dawn's Quilava|Dawn's Cyndaquil]] and {{TP|Dawn|Mamoswine}} in a practice battle as a {{p|Kirlia}} alongside Zoey's {{p|Leafeon}}.

Before the [[Sinnoh Grand Festival|Grand Festival]], Zoey evolved Kirlia into a Gallade and used him in the final round alongside Glameow against [[Dawn's Piplup]] and {{TP|Dawn|Togekiss}}, where he narrowly gave Zoey the victory.

Gallade's known moves are {{m|Magical Leaf}}, {{m|Psychic}}, {{m|Signal Beam}}, {{m|Psycho Cut}}, {{m|Swords Dance}}, and {{m|Vacuum Wave}}.}}

{{TrainerPoké
|trainer=Zoey
|pkmn=Leafeon
|type1=Grass
|img=Zoey Leafeon.png
|epnum=DP155
|epname=Double-Time Battle Training!
|vajp=Chinami Nishimura
|vaen=Chinami Nishimura
|desc={{p|Leafeon}} first appeared in ''[[DP155|Double-Time Battle Training!]]'', where it defeated [[Dawn's Quilava|Dawn's Cyndaquil]] and {{TP|Dawn|Mamoswine}} in a practice battle alongside Zoey's Kirlia.

Leafeon later reappeared in ''[[DP176|Coming Full Festival Circle!]]'' where it was used by Zoey in the semifinals of the [[Sinnoh Grand Festival]] alongside her {{p|Mismagius}} where it went up against [[Nando's Kricketune]] and {{p|Lopunny}}. Although Leafeon was weakened by Kricketune's {{m|Bug Buzz}} it was eventually able to strike back thanks to an incredible {{m|Aerial Ace}}-{{m|Psywave}} combination move that allowed it to grow wings and hit back with {{m|Leaf Blade}}. Although it was a hard fought competition, Leafeon and Mismagius were able to secure Zoey her place in the final.

Leafeon's known moves are {{m|Magical Leaf}}, {{m|Leaf Blade}}, {{m|Double Team}}, {{m|Energy Ball}}, and {{m|Aerial Ace}}.}}
